NOTES: Harvest-objective permits: Biologists set harvest objectives/quotas for certain bear units. When the objective is met, the unit closes to bear hunting for the rest of the season. A harvest-objective permit allows an eligible hunter to use any legal weapon to harvest one black bear on any open harvest-objective unit until the season ends or that unit objective is met. The permit is available to residents and nonresidents beginning March 10, 2026 at 8 a.m. MDT through wildlife.utah.gov, license agents, or DWR offices. A harvest-objective permit purchased after the season opens is not valid until three days from purchase; the last day to purchase a 2026 harvest-objective permit is Nov. 5, 2026. Hunters may not use bait with a harvest-objective permit. Fall harvest-objective hunts may include additional method restrictions such as no dogs/no bait or no bait, as listed by unit. Harvest-objective permits are available to eligible hunters except those who drew a bear limited-entry permit, not including pursuit or restricted pursuit permits. Hunters may only obtain one permit to harvest a bear during the 2026 season. Before every hunting trip, hunters must verify the unit is still open at wildlife.utah.gov/bearharvest. DWR updates closures daily by noon, effective the following day 30 minutes before official sunrise. After a harvest-objective unit closes, the permit may still be used on other open harvest-objective units. Multiseason limited-entry permit holders may hunt open harvest-objective seasons on the unit they draw without obtaining an additional harvest-objective permit; harvest-objective closures still apply. Source: 2026 Bear Cougar Furbearer Guidebook, Utah Admin. Rule R657-33-29 through R657-33-31. NOTES: Black Bear pursuit permit - nonresident: Pursuit only; a pursuit permit authorizes pursuit, but not harvest, of black bears on units not designated as restricted during open pursuit seasons. Season dates are Spring: Mar 28-May 25, 2026; Summer: July 5-July 31, 2026; Fall: Nov 2-8, 2026. Nonresidents with pursuit permits may not use them on restricted pursuit units unless accompanying someone who possesses a valid restricted pursuit permit or hunting permit for that unit and season. See the 2026 Bear Cougar Furbearer Guidebook and hunt.utah.gov for complete unit availability.
